protocols.criteria: Suspected infection + qSOFA score >= 2 (Low BP < 100, High Resp Rate > 22, Altered Mentation)
immediate_action: Administer 30ml/kg Crystalloid for Hypotension or Lactate >= 4mmol/L
Apply Vasopressors if hypotensive during or after fluid resuscitation

protocols.criteria: Acute onset involving skin/mucosa + Resp compromise OR Low BP OR GI symptoms
immediate_action: IM Epinephrine (1:1000) immediately into mid-outer thigh
Position patient supine (legs up) unless vomiting

protocols.criteria: Chest pain/pressure, radiation to arm/jaw, diaphoresis, nausea (or atypical in women/diabetics)
immediate_action: Nitroglycerin SL (if SBP > 90 and no ED meds)

protocols.criteria: FAST: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call 911
